title: UDHEZIM ADMINISTRATIV MFPT NR. 01 2025 PER SHFRYTEZIMIN E PAJISJEVE ELEKTRONIKE FISKALE SISTEMEVE FISKALE DHE SOFTUEREVE ELEKTRONIKE FISKALE
text:
Republic of Kosovo
Republic of Kosovo - Republic of Kosovo
Government - Vlada – Government
Ministry of Finance, Labour and Transfers - Ministry of Finance,
Labour and Transfers
ADMINISTRATIVE INSTRUCTION (MFPT) NO. 01/2025 ON THE USE OF
ELECTRONIC FISCAL DEVICES, FISCAL SYSTEMS AND ELECTRONIC FISCAL
SOFTWARE

Minister of Finance, Labor and Transfers,
Pursuant to Article 121 of Law No. 08/L-257 on the Administration of Tax Procedures, and
Article 11 of Law No. 08/L-117 on the Government of the Republic of Kosovo,
Approves:
ADMINISTRATIVE INSTRUCTION (MFPT) NO. 01/2025 ON THE USE OF
ELECTRONIC FISCAL DEVICES, FISCAL SYSTEMS AND ELECTRONIC FISCAL
SOFTWARE
CHAPTER I
GENERAL PROVISIONS
Article 1
Purpose
1. This Administrative Instruction (hereinafter: the Instruction) determines the rules, criteria,
obligations, deadlines and procedures that must be followed regarding the issuance of fiscal
coupons during the performance of economic activities in the territory of the Republic of
Kosovo, as well as the means to encourage consumers to obtain fiscal coupons.
2. The provisions of this Instruction guarantee the continuity of the functioning of Electronic
Fiscal Devices/Fiscal Systems prior to the entry into force of this Instruction.
Article 2
Scope
The provisions of this Instruction are mandatory for the Tax Administration of Kosovo, taxpayers,
manufacturers/developers and maintainers of Electronic Fiscal Devices/Fiscal Systems and
Electronic Fiscal Software, as well as for all other entities involved in the fiscalization process
and other issues included in this Instruction.
Article 3

ABBREVIATIONS
1. For purposes of this Instruction the following abbreviations have the following meanings:
1.1. 2D Barcode – Electronic signature generated through encryption of data in the
Fiscal Document;
1.2. FCR – Fiscal Cash Register;
1.3. API – Application Programming Interface;
1.4. KBRA – Kosovo Business Registration Agency;
1.5. TAK – Tax Administration of Kosovo;
1.6. FCRD – Fiscal Cash Register Division;
1.7. FD – Fiscal Document, like any document printed by EFD/FS/EFS, which is
signed with a 2D barcode encrypted by the TAK Server;
1.8. E-POS - Electronic Point of Sale;
1.9. EPROM – Erasable Programmable Read Only Memory);
1.10. GPRS – Standard for wireless communication (General Packet Radio Service);
1.11. JSON – JavaScript Object Notation;
1.12. CC – Crypto Card, secure media for storing ciphers (encryption /decryption
certificate);
1.13. MC – Memory Card;
1.14. SCM – Safe Crypto Module, (data encryption);
1.15. QR Code – A two-dimensional quick response barcode used to easily and quickly
access information;
1.16. MFLT – Ministry of Finance, Labor and Transfers;
1.17. UINFV – Unique Identification Number of Fiscal Coupon;
1.18. EO – Economic Operator authorized for the supply, installation, verification,
repair and maintenance of EFE/FS;
1.19. PC – Personal computer;

1.20. PDF – Portable Document Format;
1.21. EFE/FS – Electronic Fiscal Equipment/Fiscal System;
1.22. FP – Fiscal Printer;
1.23. PKI – Public Key Infrastructure;
1.24. POS – Point of Sale;
1.25. Protobuf – Protocol Data Format (Protocol Buffers);
1.26. PROM – Programmable ROM ( Programmable Read Only Memory );
1.27. ROM – Read Only Memory;
1.28. EFS – Electronic Fiscal Software;
1.29. TCP/IP – Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol;
1.30. IT – Information Technology;
1.31. TT – Tax Terminal;
1.32. VPN – Virtual Private Network;
1.33. ECJ– Electronic Control Journal (memory card); and
1.34. EFSD – Electronic Fiscal Software Developer.
Article 4
Definitions
1. For purposes of this Instruction, the following definitions have the following meanings and
contents:
1.1. Electronic Fiscal Equipment / Fiscal System - a computerized electronic
equipment or system used to issue sales receipts for retail transactions. EFE/FS
includes those electronic devices, such as: fiscal cash registers, fiscal printers and
fiscal systems for special activities. A EFE/FS includes fiscal memory, daily
working memory, firmware, accurate time, coupon printing mechanism, as well as
connecting control units for keyboards, TT, SCM, MC, ECJ, displays and input
and output data transmission sub-units (such as scanners), which are provided to
be part of the system. All components provided and functions of EFE/FS are
approved or authorized by FCRD, while the supporting software (firmware) or

other verification mechanism of the functions of EFE/FS must be at the disposal
of TAK;
1.2. Electronic Fiscal Software - Electronic software developed to record and report
taxpayers' financial transactions in an automated manner and in accordance with
tax legislation in the Republic of Kosovo. Through EFS, supplies of goods and/or
services for retail transactions are recorded, a fiscal coupon is generated and data
is reported in real time to the TAK information system;
1.3. Fiscal cash registers - are electronic recorders of the supply of goods and/or
services, cash registers that are specially equipped with fiscal memory, fiscal seals
and other options and equipment as defined in the technical and functional
specifications published by TAK;
1.4. Fiscal memory - is a stored and secure medium, it is a sub-unit in which all fiscal
information is stored and kept unchangeable in the EFE/FS. Fiscal memory is
programmed as read-only memory (read-only, EPROM, erasable programmed
ROM, or PROM, programmed ROM). Fiscal memory circuits are protected in a
special box which is located in a specially designed part, which is an integral part
of the PEF housing. The fiscal memory also includes a component (casing) that
records any removal or intervention in the EFE/FS housing;
1.5. Fiscal data network - is the communication network between the EFE/FS and the
TAK Information System that deals with the encrypted transmission of fiscal data
through the KMS;
1.6. Fiscal Cash Register Division – A division established within the TAK
Organizational Structure, responsible for the management of EFE/FS/FES, to
review and approve EFE/FS, economic operators for the supply, installation and
maintenance of EFE/FS and Authorized EFE/FS Technicians;
1.7. End consumer – means a non-business natural person who uses the purchased
goods and/or services for personal needs. Also, a final consumer may be
considered a business natural person, legal person, partnership, and grouping or
association of persons including consortia and public authorities who uses the
purchased goods and/or services for business needs and not for the purpose of
resale or development of their activities;
1.8. Fiscal coupon – is a document in which the supply of goods and/or services at the
point of sale to the final consumer is recorded. The fiscal coupon is generated by
the EFE/FS/FES, which is printed on paper or can be sent electronically for
specific supplies (electronic supplies). The specific characteristics of the fiscal
coupon are: fiscal logo and signature with 2D barcode (QR Code). Other
characteristics will be defined in the document of specific technical and functional
requirements for the EFE/FS/FES;

1.9. Firmware - Software built for the EFE to operate;
1.10. Carcass - Memory or special space within the fiscal memory for recording
interventions in the EFE/FS lodge;
1.11. . TAK Information System - Computer system that receives/sends commands
and encrypted data to traffic recording systems;
1.12. Subscription - Periodic fee that enables data transfer from EFE/FS to the TAK
Information System via GPRS technology;
1.13. Internet - A global interconnected network of computers and other electronic
devices that uses standard communication protocols (such as TCP/IP) to exchange
information; and
1.14. Payment method - Payment via cash, debit card, credit card, bank account, check,
SMS or any other equivalent payment instrument.
2. Other terms used in this Instruction have the same meaning as in the applicable Law on the
Administration of Tax Procedures (hereinafter: the Law).
CHAPTER II
OBLIGATION TO ISSUE A FISCAL COUPON
Article 5
Persons who must issue fiscal coupons
1. All persons who supply goods and/or services to the final consumer, regardless of the
method of payment, issue fiscal coupons for those services.
2. Notwithstanding paragraph 1 of this Article, a person who provides free supplies of goods
and/or services to the end consumer, for tax purposes, is obliged to issue a fiscal receipt for
that supply.
3. Exceptionally to paragraph 1 of this Article, all persons who supply goods and/or services
to natural persons, legal entities and other persons (except the final consumer when they
must issue fiscal coupons) shall use the tax invoice, as defined by the tax legislation in force.
4. The relevant Minister of Finance, in addition to the exceptions set out in paragraph 3 of this
article, may, by special decision, exempt any other category of taxpayers from the obligation
to issue a fiscal coupon.

Article 6
The moment of issuing the fiscal coupon
1. Printing and giving the fiscal coupon to the consumer is mandatory at the moment of
payment for the supply of goods and/or services .
2. Printing and issuing a fiscal receipt to the consumer is also mandatory for any payment made
before the supply of goods or before the completion of the supply of services.
3. Exceptionally to paragraph 1 of this Article, in cases where the supply of goods and/or
services occurs, but payment is made later (with a payment term) as agreed by both parties,
the printing and issuance of a fiscal receipt to the consumer is mandatory at the time of supply
of the goods and/or services.
Article 7
Contents of the fiscal coupon
1. The fiscal coupon is compiled based on the specific and technical requirements for
EFE/FS/FES approved by the Director General of TAK.
2. The fiscal coupon contains, at least, the following information:
2.1 Unique Identification Number/Fiscal Number of the taxpayer;
2.2 VAT number, if the taxpayer is registered for VAT;
2.3 Name of the taxpayer;
2.4 Taxpayer’s address;
2.5 The name of the person (operator) and his/her identification number;
2.6 At least one line for each item :
2.6.1 quantity;
2.6.2 unit price;
2.6.3 the name of the item or service;
2.6.4 the value of the supply quantity;

2.6.5 the relevant VAT rate marked with a specific letter for each item supplied;
2.7 The percentage (or value) of the discount and its amount with a minus sign or the
percentage (or value) of the increase, if any, per item;
2.8 The amount of the subtotal, the percentage (or value) of the discount on it and its
amount with a minus sign, the percentage (or value) of the surplus, if any;
2.9 Total amount for payment;
2.10 The amount paid in various payment methods, such as: cash, credit card, debit card;
bank account, coupon; check, SMS, etc.;
2.11 The amount of tax according to different active tax rates;
2.12 Total amount excluding tax;
2.13 Date, hour and minutes of release;
2.14 EFE/FS/FES identification number;
2.15 Daily fiscal coupon number;
2.16 QR code;
2.17 Unique Identification Number of the Fiscal Coupon (for verification of the fiscal
coupon);
2.18 Logo – RKS;
2.19 The type of coupon (regular, cancellation, return, etc.); and
2.20 Free text, etc.
CHAPTER III
ELECTRONIC FISCAL EQUIPMENT AND FISCAL SYSTEMS
Article 8
Persons obliged to install Electronic Fiscal Equipment and/or Fiscal Systems
1. The persons obliged for EFE/FSA are as follows:

1.1 Taxpayers defined in Article 5 of this Instruction, who exercise economic
activity, in retail sales, in the provision of services in places or units open to the
public or any other similar activity, are obliged to use the EFE/FS and other
mandatory devices for the registration and verification of the supply of goods
and/or services.
1.2 In the event of any change in the use of the EFE/FS (location, sale, transfer,
deregistration, cessation of economic activity or similar), the EFE/FS user must
notify TAK, in accordance with the procedures established by TAK.
2. Pursuant to paragraph 1 of this Article, the following categories aren’t obliged:
2.1. Enterprises that provide energy services, utility services, telecommunications
services, banking and/or financial services (including money transfer services,
microfinance institution services, financial leasing services), as well as other
public enterprises that, for goods sold and/or services provided, are paid
compensation by consumers based on consumption calculation invoices through
measuring instruments (electricity, heating, water, telephone) or any other method
of billing, such as waste management companies, etc.;
2.2 Activities of public government institutions, including but not limited to artistic
and literary creations, theatrical arts activities, activities of artistic institutions,
library, archive and museum activities;
2.3 A person (business) who provides supplies of goods and/or services only to a
person engaged in economic activity (business). This person is obliged to issue a
tax invoice, as defined by the tax legislation in force.
3. The person to whom the obligation specified in paragraph 2 of this article refers and who
simultaneously carries out activities that are not exempted or exempt from the obligation of
registration through EFE/FS and of data transfer through the terminal to the TAK Information
system, within the meaning of paragraph 1 of this article, will be obliged to register each
individual transaction during the performance of these activities through EFE/FS, as well as to
transfer them the records through terminal to TAK server.
4. The relevant Minister of Finance, in addition to the exceptions set out in paragraph 2 of this
article, may, by special decision, exempt any other category of taxpayers from the obligation to
install a EFE/FS.
Article 9
Types of Electronic Fiscal Equipment and Fiscal Systems
1. The general description of the EFE/FS is as follows:

1.1. The EFE model includes the Fiscal Cash Register (FCR), the Fiscal Printer (FP)
as well as Electronic Point of Sale Systems, connected devices, data transmission
devices and systems that include:
1.1.1. Non-portable Fiscal Cash Register “stand-alone” or independent and
independent portable, and Points Systems Electronic Point of Sale Systems
for recording and storing data in accordance with the decision of the General
Director of TAK;
1.1.2. The Fiscal Cash Register has intangible fiscal memory and working memory;
and
1.1.3. The data to be recorded and stored in memory (working memory) and in the
fiscal memory) are related to the goods sold and services provided to
consumers, by printing the fiscal report, as required by the legislation in force;
1.2. The fiscal printer is a EFE with all the relevant features and is managed through an
application software and can be used as:
1.2.1. Personal computer (PC) based POS;
1.2.2. A separate electronic fiscal system with fiscal memory for recording retail sales
turnover for:
1.2.2.1. Derivate of petroleum through computing devices/systems for the sale
of liquid fuels (gas pumps, gasoline, and similar);
1.2.2.2. Car wash, parking, transportation, and other services; and
1.2.2.3. Special categories that may be determined later by decision of the
Director General of TAK.
2. Any other device including other fiscal systems, which will be specified by a sub-legal act
approved by the Minister, including technical and functional requirements and the time of
commencement of their implementation.
Article 10
The definition EFE types
1. EFE/s are equipment with fiscal memory and working memory, in which data on supplies of goods
and/or services to customers are recorded in an adequate manner, so as to enable printing,
as well as to enable the transfer and further processing of this data, as described in this
Instruction.
2. EFE/s must operate in such a way that general data is stored for each transaction in the fiscal
memory and memory card, preventing any change or manipulation of the data before,

during and after the preservation at the fiscal memory. These data are transmitted to TAK
as a daily summary.
3. EFE/s should have a special parameter to regulate the status of multilingualism, in
accordance with the legislation into force on the use of official languages in the Republic
of Kosovo.
4. There will be following types of EFE/s authorized to operate in Kosovo, which may be
classified as follows:
4.1. Portable Fiscal Cash register;
4.2. Non-portable Fiscal Cash register; and
4.3. Electronic Point of Sale System, with authorized components such as fiscal printers,
electronic fiscal devices to be installed and integrated with the existing point of sales
systems.
Article 11
Portable and Non-Portable Fiscal Cash Registers
1. The portable and non-portable cash register through which the circulation of goods and/or
services is recorded should have the following functions:
1.1. The portable cash register must have appropriate dimensions and have installed
rechargeable batteries, which enable printing at least ten thousand (10,000) lines
without recharging of batteries. Such an FCR is not required to have a consumer
monitor;
1.2. The portable cash register must have an incorporated TT or an interface for connecting
and using an external TT;
1.3. The non-portable cash register must have a customer monitor, interface to control
withdrawal of cash and at least one interface for connecting to a barcode scanner;
1.4. Every EFE should have a specific serial number which is registered with TAK as well
as serial number of fiscal memory.
2. The cash register can be connected to a computer, either directly or through a computer
network.
3. No computer software is allowed to change the data in fiscal memory.

4. The fiscal cash register must have software with an independent fiscal module protected
from changes and deletions, using standard functions for registering goods and/or services,
displaying and managing data entry in the fiscal memory.
5. The cash register must have the ability to print a fiscal coupon for supplies made to each
customer.
6. The cash register must have the ability to record all daily transactions, maintained in an
electronic journal of accounts as provided for in this Instruction.
7. The cash register should have the opportunity to print management reports without deletion,
such as: “report X” (cash in the drawer), “report Z” (daily closing report) as well as various
periodic reports.
8. The cash register should have the opportunity to cancel items on the receipt before printing,
while returning items is done through a return/cancellation receipt (refund).
9. An FCR may also have additional equipment such as: barcode scanning devices and credit
card payment terminals or other equipment that may be authorized by a special decision by
the Director General of TAK.
Article 12
Point of Sale System with Fiscal Printer as a Coupon Printer
1. Point of sale fiscal systems should only use the fiscal printer. In such a system, the fiscal
printer should be connected to the control unit at the POS. It is typical and much more
flexible for the control unit to be a computer guided by a software program.
2. Each fiscal printer in the system must have a unique serial number, which is registered in
TAK as well as serial number of fiscal memory.
3. Point of sale (POS) systems are different, the functional and technical specifications will
provide for the same obligations and liabilities with which they are charged. users of Fiscal
Cash Registers, but NOT WILL to mandate WAY that HOW WILL THE these obligations
are fulfilled.
Article 13
Embedded Software and Hardware
1. The embedded hardware includes the microprocessor or microcontroller, as well as other
components. The embedded software, called “firmware,” acts as a fiscal data management
system and also acts as a control program for the equipment.

2. The software and hardware used by the EFE user must transfer fiscal encrypted data in
required format through SCM and TT in the TAK server.
3. The installation of any EFE, by an authorized person, is done in a way that prevents changes
to the original authorized software and hardware. Once the software and hardware are
installed, any modification is done only by an authorized technician.
Article 14
The External Application Software included in POS Fiscal Systems
1. The external application software included in the POS fiscal system supports the rest of the
system functions for data management (accounting and similar).
2. In cases where the application software is linked to the content of fiscal coupons, the
authorized person who performs the installation, together with the user, as well as the
authorized person for the POS software, provide written confirmation to TAK that the
software functions properly in accordance with the specifications and conditions for data
security. Testing, which must be carried out before the EFE is in function, must confirm
that the software that generates fiscal coupons operates in full compliance with the tax legal
requirements for books and registers, with the requirements of this Instruction, with the
approved technical and functional specifications by the General Director of TAK, and that
does not violate in any manner the integrity of the EFE fiscal data.
3. Testing that confirms in writing the normal functioning of the software includes:
3.1. Full details on the application software manufacturer;
3.2. The exact distribution name and current software version number;
3.3. A copy of the demonstration version of the specific software stored in an appropriate
format and medium; and
3.4. Complete data on software requirements and operating conditions in computer;
4. The operation of the EFE with an interconnected system that uses unauthorized application
software for issuing fiscal coupons, which is prohibited and subject to punishment as
provided by the relevant legislation;
5. The functional and technical specifications of software in special cases are determined by
decision of the Director General of TAK.

Article 15
The Communications Network
1. For the electronic transmission of fiscal data at predetermined intervals, a communication
network between EFE/FS and TAK through SCM and TT is established.
2. The TAK Information System can establish communication (on demand) with the SCM and
EFE/FS and requires:
2.1. Daily report;
2.2. The total of fiscal coupons fiscal; and
2.3. Other information required by TAK.
3. The fiscal data communication networks between the EFE/FS and TAK must be:
3.1. Safe – all connections must operate on Virtual Private Networks (VPN), between TT
and the TAK computer system, connected via the internet. Telecommunications
providers are responsible for implementing and maintaining the VPN topology and
VPN operation through GPRS technology. They support any TT with SIM card and a
dynamic address. TT should be reconfigured to work with only one EFE/FS;
3.2. The Electronic Fiscal Equipment communicates with the TAK Information
System within a schedule at the time set by the TAK Information System through
the SCM. Also, the TAK Information System can at any time establish
communication with the EFE/FS/SCM;
3.3. Efficient – no unnecessary traffic or journey is allowed unless specified from the
TAK Information System. Network traffic should be allocated in a manner that
will reduce costs for TAK and the taxpayer, while simultaneously meeting all
specified requirements; and
3.4. Compatible – EFE/FS and transmitting devices must be compatible with all
communication protocols and standards. Communication with the TAK
Information System via TCP/IP socket according to specific technical and
functional requirements required by TAK.
4. Telecommunications providers must be authorized by the competent authority to provide
services for the transfer of fiscal data from EFE/FS to the TAK system, according to
specific technical and functional requirements determined by TAK.

Article 16
Information System Integrated in TAK
1. The TAK IT system must accept encrypted (encrypted) fiscal data and other data
transmitted by the EFE/FS from all taxpayers connected to the TAK system.
2. The IT system will automatically process the information received from the EFE/FS for
each taxpayer.
3. The frequency of data transmission from the EFE/FS to the TAK Information System will
be determined by decision of the TAK Director General.
Article 17
Fiscal Cash Register Division
1. The Fiscal Cash Division, which operates within the TAK, is tasked with:
1.1. Development and implementation of testing procedures and approval of EFE/FS and
other equipment, including all models and types of EFE/FS to be authorized for use in
the Republic of Kosovo, based on a review of the manufacturer’s specifications to
determine whether they meet the specific technical and functional requirements of
defined for EFE/FS;
1.2. Reviewing the requests of potential economic operators for the sale, installation and
maintenance of EFE/FS, to ensure that they meet the criteria set by FCRD;
1.3. Testing the proposed EFE/FS models to ensure that they meet technical and functional
specifications, as well as international EC standards;
1.4. Determining rules for implementing safety obligations and methods for supervising
technicians regarding the installation and repair of EFE/FS;
1.5. The Director General of TAK may assign to the FCRD any other function that he
considers necessary for the proper functioning of the EFE/FS system.
2. Development of procedures for application, authorization to sell, install and repair EFE/FS
as well as the internal procedures and functioning of this Division, will be determined by the
job description and based on TAK strategic documents.
Article 18
Authorizations of Economic Operators
1. Economic Operators, authorized according to the legal provisions before the entry into force

of this Instruction, are considered to be authorized to carry out the process of fiscalization of
taxpayers with EFE/FS, until the end of the transitional period, or until the period determined
by another decision by the relevant Minister of Finance.
2. The Economic Operator is obliged to support with spare parts and maintain the EFE/FS
which are in use by taxpayers for at least five (5) years from the last sale date of EFE/FS in the
Kosovo market or until the end of the transitional period, or until the period determined by
another decision by the relevant Minister of Finance.
3. If a EFE/FS model is not re-certified by the EO for any reason, then this model is not eligible
to be sold by the EO and by taxpayer as EFE/FS.
4. Electronic Fiscal Equipment which has been used by the same taxpayer for five (5) years,
and which furthermore is not supported by spare parts by EO, cannot be used as EFE/FS.
5. Exclusion from paragraph 4 of this Article, the EFE/FS which, even after five (5) years of
use, meets the specific technical and functional requirements and is supported by the EO, may
be used as a EFE/FS until the end of the transitional period, or until the period determined by
another decision by the relevant Minister of Finance.
ARTICLE 19
Application about Economic Operator and EFE/FS
1. Any manufacturer, importer or assembler of EFE/FS, can become an authorized EFE/FS
Operator, for one or more specific models at any time according to criteria published by TAK.
2. The application to be an EO is submitted at any time, but the sale of electronic fiscal
equipment can only be made after approval by the FCRD.
3. EFE/FS certification is carried out by competent and authorized Institutes, Laboratories or
Institutions for testing and verifying compliance with specific technical and functional
requirements determined by TAK.
4. Authorization of competent Institutes, Laboratories or Institutions is done based on the
criteria stipulated by ATK.
5. Installation and maintenance of the EFE/FS is carried out only by EO personnel. who sold
EFE/FS.
6. The Fiscal Cash Register Division is responsible for requesting and verifying any
information and data, including: evidence of experience, solvency, credibility, technical
personnel and similar data.
7. The Fiscal Cash Register Division, within thirty (30) days from the date of receipt of the

requests and information, decides whether or not to grant authorization to the EO.
8. The Fiscal Cash Register Division is responsible for withdrawing and canceling the
authorization granted in the event that:
8.1. Users face serious problems;
8.2. The Economic Operator has violated any provision of this or any other sub-legal act
issued in relation to the EFE/FS;
8.3. In cases where the EO fails to certify at least one model of EFE/FS by the competent
authority within twelve (12) months from the date of receipt of authorization from
TAK;
8.4. The Economic Operator does not submit tax statements or does not fulfill tax
obligations on time even after the request sent by TAK;
8.5. In the event of a change in the conditions under which the authorization was granted,
which are not updated by the Economic Operator.
9. An appeal may be filed against the FCRD decision in the competent court.
10. Periodic tests, upon request, through checking for proper operation of the equipment may
be carried out by FCRD, or by designated institutions, laboratories or organizations authorized
by this Division, to ensure that after initial approval, the EFE/FS continue to meet the relevant
requirements and operate reliably even after installation at points of sale.
11. The Kosovo Tax Administration is entitled to review any installation of EFE/FS completed
by any Operator, to ensure that it works in accordance with established requirements and
specifications. In addition, TAK has the right to review registrations of any operator to be
assured that it meets all the requirements set out in relation with authorization to act as a
supplier, installer and maintainer of EFE/FS .
12. The Kosovo Tax Administration, with the approval of the relevant Minister of Finance,
determines the basic conditions, procedures that must be followed and documents that must be
submitted when requesting authorization for the supply, installation and maintenance of EFE/FS. In
this document, in addition to other requirements that TAK may impose, it must request that:
12.1. The Economic Operator shall guarantee the work with a bank guarantee, or other
forms of acceptable guarantee;
12.2. The Economic Operator must be re-licensed every four (4) years by TAK;
12.3. The Economic Operator must have the certified EFE/FS models re-certified by the
competent authority every four (4) years;

12.4. For any failure to comply with the conditions set by TAK during the performance of
its activities in the sale, installation and repair of EFE/FS, the EO will be directly
responsible and will be subject to the legal responsibilities provided for by applicable
legislation;
12.5. For control purposes by TAK, the EO prepares a user manual for accessing and
extracting information from the EFE/FS;
12.6. The Economic Operator must ensure that telecommunications between the EFE/FS
and the TAK information system are carried out through a telecommunication
operator authorized by the competent authority;
12.7. The Authorized Economic Operator enters into an agreement with the authorized
telecommunications operator which contains, among other things, the appropriate
guarantee for continuous telecommunications capabilities between the EFE/FS and
the information system at TAK; and
12.8. The EO’s agreement with the authorized telecommunications company must provide
guaranteed replacement or repair of their own communication equipment (modem)
and similar equipment), within forty-eight (48) hours after the problem has been
reported;
13. The Economic Operator is obliged to respond to all requests from taxpayers to be equipped
with EFE/FS within a maximum period of fifteen (15) days from the date of submission of the
request for supply of equipment.
14. The Economic Operator is obliged to intervene and eliminate the malfunction of the EFE/FS
according to the requests of taxpayers and TAK for servicing and maintenance of the EFE/FS,
within a period not later than forty-eight (48) hours from the moment the problem appears.
Article 20
Issuance of the unique EFE/FS certification number
After successfully passing all tests for the specific model of equipment, FCRD issues the EO
with a unique protected authorization number. This authorization is proof to the buyer that that
EO is authorized to sell that equipment and that it meets all specifications and conditions for
use as a EFE/FS. FCRD based on the testing results should issue a decision for certification of
the relevant equipment within fifteen (15) days after receiving the report from the competent
and authorized Institutes, Laboratories or Institutions for testing and verification of compliance
with the specific technical and functional requirements determined by TAK.

Article 21
Maintenance of data related to EO, installed EFE/FS and users
1. The Kosovo Tax Administration maintains electronic records of data for all installed
EFE/FS, which include:
1.1. identification of the installed device, type of EFE/FS, serial number of EFE/FS, serial
number of fiscal memory, serial number of KK, manufacturer and model of EFE/FS
and other data described in the technical specifications;
1.2. the date when the EFE/FS was installed and when it was approved by TAK;
1.3. name, address and unique identification number/fiscal number of the user;
1.4. the place where the EFE/FS is installed;
1.5. the name of the EO, its address, unique identification number/fiscal number and VAT
number of the authorized person;
1.6. the authorization number issued to the EO, for the specific type of EFE/FS;
1.7. data on persons authorized by the EO who supply, install and maintain the EFE/FS;
1.8. persons authorized to repair the EFE/FS, including the type of EFE/FS that the person
is authorized to repair, his/her name and address, card identification number, personal
identification number and expiration date of the authorization; and
1.9. the authorization number issued for each specific EFE/FS.
2. The Kosovo Tax Administration maintains and updates the database for electronic records.
Article 22
Authorized technicians
1. The Authorized Economic Operator appoints technicians who install and repair the EFE/FS,
for which it is authorized to sell. The installation of the authorized EFE/FS must be carried
out only by the designated technician. The authorized EO may contract third parties who are
not employed by it to maintain the EFE/FS. However, this does not relieve the EO of its
responsibility to ensure that the maintenance is carried out correctly and in accordance with
the established procedures.

2. The Authorized Economic Operator shall submit to the FCRD the names, addresses and
personal identification numbers of the technicians before they are authorized to install and
repair the EFE/FS. The Authorized Operator shall include all necessary company and personal
data (including technical qualifications), as well as a photo identification document.
3. The Fiscal Cash Register Division reviews the data submitted by the authorized EO, and may
request necessary clarifications, before issuing or refusing authorization, for technicians who
will install or repair the EFE/FS. The FCRD may request:
3.1. A statement that the individual is authorized by the EO and certified by the manufacturer
to install and maintain the designated EFE/FS; and
3.2. List of types of EFE/FS that the technician is authorized to install or maintain.
4. Upon approval of the technician, FCRD will request from the relevant institution to provide
an identification card with a photograph that identifies this individual as an installation and
maintenance technician. The technician is provided with a unique identification number
which will be compared with his/her personal identification number in the database
maintained by TAK, described in this Instruction.
5. The Identification Card contains:
5.1. Name and surname of the technician;
5.2. Unique identification number (stamp);
5.3. Dates until which the identification card is valid.
6. The Fiscal Cash Register Division may also authorize other technicians to maintain the
EFE/FS. The person seeking authorization as a maintenance technician shall submit an
application to the FCRD before performing any maintenance on the EFE/FS. The application
for authorization shall include: name, address, personal identification number, employment
history, technical experience, and a photo identification document. Attached to the application
shall be a certificate from the authorized EO stating that he is technically capable of
maintaining the EFE/FS, copies of diplomas or certificates for technical training and
knowledge of the EFE/FS.
7. The Fiscal Cash Register Division reviews the request and makes a decision. In case of
rejection of the request, a written notification of the reasons for the rejection shall be made.
The request shall be rejected only if the documentation is incomplete, or the applicant does
not meet the conditions to be an authorized maintenance technician. If the applicant’s request
as a maintenance technician is approved, the FCRD shall implement the procedures of
paragraph 4 of this Article.

8. The validity of the authorization for installation and maintenance technicians shall be three
(3) years. After this period, an application for renewal of the authorization shall be made.
Maintenance and installation technicians shall submit to DAF an application for renewal at
least forty-five (45) days before the expiration of the existing authorization. The application
for renewal shall include the data and certificates required under the conditions in force at the
time of the new application. The continuation of the authorization shall be approved by DAF,
unless there is evidence that the technician has any technical or legal impediment to the
installation and maintenance of EFE/FS.
9. If a technician authorized for installation and maintenance of EFE/FS, ceases the activity for
which he/she is authorized, before the expiration of the authorization, the EO notifies TAK
(revoke the authorization and return the identification card) within thirty (30) days after the
cessation of the activity.
Article 23
Security of access within the EFE/FS
1. The safety of access to the interior of the EFE/FS is protected by a special nut (bolt), which
connects the upper part of the EFE/FS to the lower part. This nut is placed on the visible part
of the EFE/FS housing cover so that intervention inside the EFE/FS is impossible without
removing the protective nut (bolt).
2. The security seal - the seal, is used to seal the nut (bolt) which does not allow scratching and
is made in such a way that it is impossible to remove without damaging it.
3. If a technician or authorized EO discovers that the seal seal has been damaged on the EFE/FS,
the technician must immediately report the case to the relevant unit at TAK. The technician
shall not make any intervention until authorized by TAK.
4. If a TAK employee identifies a damaged seal, he or she shall immediately notify the
responsible TAK official to remove the EFE/FS from the power supply and not use it until it
has been examined and determined to be functioning normally.
Article 24
Authorized technicians - access control code
1. The opening and re-sealing of a EFE/FS is carried out only by a technician authorized by the
FCRD in accordance with Article 21 of this Instruction.

2. The authorized technician applies relevant configurations with a EFE/FS software program,
using a special algorithm, access code and password.
Article 25
Installation, control, maintenance of EFE/FS
1. The Economic Operator must ensure that each installed EFE/FS operates without any hindrance and
in accordance with the specific technical and functional requirements for EFE/FS, determined
by TAK Information System.
2. Each EFE/FS must be accompanied by an installation, control, and maintenance log provided
by the EO.
3. Before installing the EFE/FS, the EO must inform TAK about the EFE/FS purchaser,
providing data such as:
3.1. The buyer’s Unique Identification Number;
3.2. The buyer’s Fiscal Number (if there is no Unique Identification Number);
3.3. The serial number of the EFE/FS to be installed;
3.4. The type of EFE/FS to be installed; and
3.5. Expected installation date.
4. During the installation of the EFE/FS, the technician conducts a communication test to ensure
that the EFE/FS is able to establish the TT communication connection with the ATK server.
5. After installing the EFE/FS, the Economic Operator reports electronically to TAK about the
installed EFE/FS, including sales invoice data, device functionality, and the date of
installation.
6. The Economic Operator must not program the EFE/FS or influence it in other ways that hinder
or block the functionality of the EFE/FS at certain periods or deadlines.
7. The authorized technician is obliged to record in the diary detailed information for each
installation, control, maintenance, of the EFE/FS, including the start and end time of the
installation, control and maintenance process, as well as the data described in his identification
card.

8. After maintenance of the EFE/FS, which had a defect, the repair technician submits to TAK
a report describing the defect, the date and time the defect occurred, and the date and time the
EFE/FS was returned to service.
9. After installation, inspection, and maintenance of each EFE/FS, the authorized technician
must verify the functionality of the EFE/FS.
10. Each EFE/FS sold and installed must undergo a regular technical inspection on a regular basis
every twenty-four (24) months. The regular inspection report and any other inspection must
be sent to TAK, in electronic form, within five (5) days, after the inspection has been carried
out. The Crypto Card, without the need for changes, must be functional and maintain the
system security standard.
11. The EFE/FS user is obliged to keep the journal specified in paragraph 2 of this article and
submit it to TAK upon request.
12. Each time the taxpayer requests it, the installed EFE/FS must be subject to inspection by the
EO, in order to provide maintenance service and avoid malfunctioning of the device.
13. The Economic Operator is obliged to intervene and eliminate the malfunction of the EFE/FS
according to the requests of taxpayers and TAK for servicing and maintenance of the EFE/FS,
within a period not later than forty-eight (48) hours from the moment the problem appears.
14. The Kosovo Tax Administration has the right to review any installation, inspection or
maintenance of the EFE/FS, as well as the journal entries made by the authorized technician,
to ensure that the installation, inspection and maintenance, as well as the entries, have been
made in accordance with the requirements and conditions set by the TAK.
Article 26
Transfer of EFE/FS
1. Each authorized EO may take over the supply, installation and maintenance of the EFE/FS of another
EO, in cases where the other EO discontinues the activity related to the supply, installation
and maintenance of the EFE/FS.
2. For the implementation of paragraph 1 of this Article, the EO must have an agreement with
another EO for the transfer of rights and responsibilities for the supply and installation of
EFE/FS remaining in stock to the other EO, as well as the maintenance of EFE/FS which have
been sold and installed by the other EO.

3. For the implementation of paragraphs 1 and 2 of this Article, EO/ss shall notify TAK thirty
(30) days in advance.
4. The Authorized Economic Operator may not discontinue the activity related to the supply,
installation and maintenance of the EFE/FS if the agreement specified in paragraph 2 of this
article has not been reached.
5. By way of exception from paragraph 4 of this Article, the authorized EO may discontinue the
activity related to the supply, installation and maintenance of the EFE/FS if the implementation of
paragraph 2 of Article 18 of this Instruction has been completed.
Article 27
Use of the tax coupon
In the event of a temporary breakdown or other technical problems of the EFE/FS, the user is
obliged to issue a written tax coupon for each sale made. The tax block (in physical or electronic
form), which contains the serial numbers, will be approved by TAK. A written report is made for
all tax coupons issued during the sale as a result of the breakdown or technical problems of the
EFE/FS. After the breakdown or problem of the EFE/FS is eliminated, the user immediately
returns it to work. Details regarding the tax coupon are determined by TAK.
Article 28
Reading mode (“X” ratio)
Each EFE/FS must allow the reading and printing of data recorded in the fiscal memory via the
keyboard for a variety of statistical reports, which are for statistical purposes only, and not for tax
purposes. The conditions for the reading regime are determined by the Director General of TAK.
Article 29
Closing mode (“Z” ratio)
1. Each EFE/FS contains the conditions defined according to the functional and technical
specifications determined by TAK, as follows:
1.1. preparing the fiscal closing of daily turnover, printing the daily tax report and the data
that must be transmitted to TAK;

1.2. the possibility of reading the contents of the fiscal memory through periodic tax reports
in accordance with published specifications;
1.3. ensuring the conditions for carrying out verification for each daily tax report for the
purpose of checking the accuracy of the functioning of the fiscal memory; and
1.4. providing daily reports and other periodic tax reports that are characterized by the title of
the report accompanied by the sign “Z”, as well as containing at the end of the coupon
(in bold) the fiscal logo and the name "TAX REPORT".
2. Exception from paragraph 1, subparagraph 1.1. of this Article, in cases where the taxpayer
notifies TAK of the temporary interruption of economic activity, then for the period of
interruption (pause) the fiscal closing of the daily turnover, the printing of the daily tax report
and the data that must be transmitted to TAK are not required.
Article 30
Programming Module (P)
1. Each EFE/FS contains, among other things:
1.1. The possibility of recording in the fiscal memory the taxpayer's data, which must be
presented on the fiscal receipt;
1.2. The possibility of programming at least four (4) tax rates. Tax rates are written in the
fiscal memory. This is allowed at the time of installation or after closing the daily tax
report and transmitting the “Z” data to the ATK. There must be the possibility of
presenting the different tax rates with the letters “A” to “E”. The letter “A” means the tax
rate exempt from VAT, while the letters “B” to “E” the different forms of VAT tax rates
provided for by the VAT Law. The change of the tax rate occurs only after the daily tax
report is finalized and the relevant data has been transmitted to the relevant fiscal
memory. Changes in the fiscal memory can be made within the special content for
changing tax rates. Changes to the tax rate are carried out by the maintainer based on the
special manual for changing tax rates;
1.3. Allowing the programming of communication parameters with the TAK communication
network for the transmission of periodic tax reports; and
1.4. Allowing configuration and reading of fiscal memory.
2. The Director General of TAK will issue a decision to limit and establish the functions and
specifications for the programming regime.

Article 31
Electronic journal of fiscal coupons
1. The electronic journal of fiscal coupons stores fiscal documents in electronic form as well as
in the electronic journal (memory card).
2. The requirements and technical specifications for the electronic journal of fiscal coupons will
be approved by the Director General of TAK.
CHAPTER IV
ELECTRONIC FISCAL SOFTWARE
Article 32
Persons obliged to install Electronic Fiscal Software
1. The persons obliged to install EFS are as follows:
1.1. Taxpayers defined in Article 5 of this Instruction, who exercise economic activity, in
retail sales, in the provision of services in places or units open to the public or any other
similar activity, including public enterprises and socially owned enterprises, are obliged
to use EFS and other mandatory devices for the registration and verification of the supply
of goods and/or services; and
1.2. Public institutions that accept cash payments for provided services.
2. For the purpose of implementing paragraph 1 of this Article, the person issuing fiscal coupons
may test the fiscal coupon data and the fiscalization procedure.
3. The methodology for testing fiscal coupon data and the fiscalization procedure by the
taxpayer issuing fiscal coupons is determined by TAK.
4. In the event of any change in the use of the EFS (location, sale, transfer, deregistration,
cessation of economic activity or similar), the EFS user must notify TAK in accordance with
the procedures established by TAK.
5. Based on paragraph 1 of this Article, the following categories are not obliged:
5.1. Public institutions that provide services for which they accept payments through bank
accounts or other forms of electronic payments.

5.2. A person (business) who provides supplies of goods and/or services only to a person
engaged in economic activity (business). This person is obliged to issue a tax invoice, as
defined by the tax legislation in force.
6. The person to whom the obligation specified in paragraph 5 of this article refers and who
simultaneously carries out activities that are not exempted or exempt from the obligation to
register through EFS and transfer data to the TAK Information System, within the meaning of
paragraph 1 of this article, will be obliged to register each individual transaction during the
performance of these activities in the EFS, through which the data are transferred to the TAK
server.
7. The relevant Minister of Finance, in addition to the exceptions set out in paragraph 5 of this
Article, may, by special decision, exempt any other category of taxpayers from the obligation
to install EFS.
Article 33
Electronic Fiscal Software
1. The Electronic Fiscal Software enables the storage of each transaction in an electronic
register (database), electronically signed for each fiscal coupon, using the digital certificate,
according to Article 35 of this Instruction, and an internet connection for electronic data
exchange with the TAK Information System.
2. The Electronic Fiscal Software through which the supply of goods and/or services is recorded
must fulfill at least the following functions:
2.1. be able to electronically sign every fiscal coupon;
2.2. to be identified through a digital certificate in the TAK system;
2.3. establish a connection for secure data exchange with the TAK Information System via
the internet;
2.4. support communication via Secure Protocol Hyper Text Transfer Protocol (HTTPS);
2.5. generate and send a fiscal coupon according to the required specifications, including the
QR Code;
2.6. to be connected to a printer for printing the fiscal receipt, according to the format
predetermined by TAK;
2.7. to perform the update ( Upgrade ) as needed;

2.8. be able to send requests (messages) in TAK system stipulated by TAK; and
2.9. to strongly support linguistics, according to the legislation in force on the use of official
languages in the Republic of Kosovo.
3. The Electronic Fiscal Software, in cases of interruptions in communications with the TAK
Information System, must enable the generation of fiscal coupons and store them safely
within FES. The moment communication with the TAK Information System is restored (the
communication with the TAK Information System is reset), this information is automatically
sent to TAK Information System.
4. Electronic Fiscal Software may be suitable for use via Personal Computer (PC), Laptop,
Tablet, smart mobile phone, etc.
5. The Director General of TAK will issue a decision on the minimum functional and technical
specifications and the manner of implementation of the EFS.
Article 34
Information System of Tax Administration of Kosovo
1. The Kosovo Tax Administration Information System must enable:
1.1. secure connection for data exchange with EFS, via the internet and the “Secure Hyper
Text Transfer Protocol (HTTPS);
1.2. validation of the taxpayer’s identity through a digital certificate;
1.3. receiving real-time requests sent by EFS for any supply of goods and/or services;
1.4. receiving and storing information from each taxpayer separately;
1.5. processing data received in real time from EFS;
1.6. verification of the fiscal coupon by the citizen through a special application; and
1.7. other important functions that will be described with technical and functional
specifications.
2. The Director General of TAK will issue a decision on the technical and functional
specifications and the mode of operation of the TAK Information System.

Article 35
Digital Certificate and Private Key
1. In order to implement the fiscalization procedure of fiscal coupons, the taxpayer, for each
EFS installed at points of sale that issues fiscal coupons, must be provided with a digital
certificate, which is generated and issued by TAK or another relevant authority for issuing
digital certificates.
2. For each installed EFS, a Private Key must be generated by the taxpayer, which must be
stored at a high level of security, without compromising its compliance and confidentiality.
3. The person issuing fiscal coupons, during the process of equipping with a digital certificate
and private key for each installed EFS, must submit the following data in electronic form:
3.1. Unique Identification Number/Fiscal Number;
3.2. Unit Number;
3.3. POS Number;
3.4. Software Solution Code; and
3.5. 3Fiscalization Number, which is obtained in the TAK Electronic System
Article 36
Application and registration of SEF developers and maintainers
1. Any natural or legal person who develops or maintains a EFS for taxpayer fiscalization
needs, according to the technical and functional specifications determined by TAK, may
apply for registration and certification of that EFS to the Commission composed of TAK.
2. The developer and/or maintainer of EFS, referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, should be
authorized by the Commission, before starting to exercise the activity of providing these
software solutions and/or maintenance services in the territory of the Republic of Kosovo.
3. The request for EFS certification may be submitted at any time, but the sale and installation
of the EFS to the taxpayer can only be made after certification by the Commission.
4. The Commission, within fifteen (15) working days from the date of receipt of the request for
certification of the EFS, decides on approval or rejection of certification of EFS.

5. The conditions and procedures for applying for EFS certification and EFS maintenance are
determined by a special Commission from TAK.
6. The Commission electronically registers the certified EFS in the registry of software
developers or maintainers managed by the Commission.
7. The registration of the SEF developer according to paragraph 6 of this article does not replace
the obligation to register with the Kosovo Business Registration Agency (KBRA) or TAK,
which carries out commercial and non-commercial activities, in accordance with the relevant
legislation in force.
8. After registration, the person referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article shall be provided with
the EFS unique code. This code, together with the UIN/FN of the software developer, must
be integrated into the software solution by the software developers before delivery to the
taxpayer issuing fiscal coupons, so that the fiscal system can identify the developer and the
software solution used during each delivery of fiscal coupons in the fiscalization procedure.
9. The Commission shall send the registration code of the software solution electronically to
the person specified in paragraph 1 of this Article. This code shall be stored in the Electronic
Fiscal Software Registry managed by the Commission.
10. Every taxpayer who issues fiscal coupon, must notify TAK of the software solution used for
issuing fiscal coupons, as well as the developer and maintainer of the software solution.
11. The registration of the software developer and software solution is carried out in each
fiscalization, since the data of the software solution used is an integral part of the data for the
fiscalization of fiscal coupons. Whereas, the data on the maintainer must be recorded during
the registration of data for the place of exercise of the taxpayer/person’s activity or within
each electronic device for issuing fiscal coupons, if the taxpayer/person has different
maintainers.
12. The taxpayer/person issuing fiscal coupons may not issue fiscal coupons if the software
developer and the software solution it uses are not registered in the register of software
developers and/or certified maintainers by the Commission. For this purpose, any
taxpayer/person issuing fiscal coupons may check through the name/first name and surname
or the code/unique identification number of the software developer whether that developer
and the software solution are registered in the register.
13. The taxpayer/person issuing fiscal coupons and developer and/or software maintainer are
responsible for the functioning of software solution according to technical specifications
determined by TAK.

Article 37
Cancellation of the EFS certificate
1. The Commission is responsible for withdrawing and canceling the certificate issued to the
EFS, in the event that:
1.1. users face serious problems related to the functioning of EFS;
1.2. the EFS developer has violated any provision of this or any other by-law issued in relation
to the EFS;
1.3. in the event of a change in the conditions under which the certificate was issued, which
are not updated by the EFS developer.
2. An appeal may be filed against the Commission’s decision in the competent court.
3. The Tax Administration of Kosovo is entitled to verify any EFS installation realized by any
EFS developer, to ensure that it works in accordance with the technical and functional
specifications, as well as other necessary elements.
Article 38
Communication network between EFS and TAK Information System
1. For the electronic transmission of fiscal data using the internet, a communication network is
created between the EFS and the TAK Server.
2. Fiscal data communication networks between EFS and the TAK Information System must
be:
2.1. safe – all connections between EFS and the TAK Information System, connected via the
internet, must be encrypted and signed with the Private Key, according to technical and
functional specifications;
2.2. direct – the establishment of communication between the EFS and the TAK Information
System is done through the HTTPS protocol, using the Internet;

2.3. efficient – no unnecessary traffic or routing is allowed unless otherwise specified by
TAK. Network traffic should be assigned in a manner that will reduce traffic costs for
TAK and the taxpayer, while meeting all specified requirements; and
2.4. compatible – EFS must be compatible with all protocols and standards for
communication with the TAK Information System.
Article 39
Sales through self-service equipment (vending machines)
1. A self-service equipment (machine) that generates turnover from the sale of goods and/or
services is considered any automated equipment, where during the supply of goods and/or
services and the collection of payments no individual participates in the capacity of the seller.
Self-service equipment for fiscalization purposes are not considered to be bank ATM/s or e-
banking (electronic banking services).
2. The self-service equipment (vending machine) performs the fiscalization process by
reporting sales data at the time of sale of goods and/or services to the TAK Information
System through an internet connection.
3. Each self-service device is considered an electronic equipment for cash sales, for which the
taxpayer’s place of business must be registered and fiscal coupons must be recorded/declared
in the taxpayer's books.
4. When submitting data regarding a self-service device (vending machine) under paragraph 3
of this article, the taxpayer must indicate the address where the self-service device is located
or its closest address (geographical location) and also describe the exact location in a given
area.
5. On each self-service device (vending machine), the taxpayer issuing fiscal coupons must
place a sticker with a printed QR code containing the following data:
5.1. The Unique Identification Number of the taxpayer; and
5.2. 5EFS identification number.
6. A decision is issued by the Director General of TAK regarding the technical and functional
specifications of self-service devices (vending machines) and data exchange with the TAK
Information System.
7. If the self-service device has a temporary interruption in the communication service with the
TAK Information System, the taxpayer issuing fiscal coupons must ensure the storage of
sales data and perform the fiscalization procedure, after eliminating the problem or restoring
the connection, within forty-eight (48) hours.

8. Taxpayers operating in specific sectors of the economy (e.g. the hydrocarbon sector, car
washes, electricity supply, or similar) and making sales through self-service machines
(vending machines), must enable the printing of fiscal coupons when making sales through
these devices.
Article 40
Fiscal coupon and QR Code generation
1. The Fiscal Coupon must be printed on paper for all supplies of goods and/or services made
to final consumers (individuals). However, if electronic services are provided or the supply
of goods and/or services is contracted and charged through a mobile application, online store
or similar, the fiscal coupon may not be printed on paper, but may be sent to the buyer or
recipient of the fiscal coupon, in electronic form. For example, in the case of distance selling
if the customer (an individual) pays by credit card, then this is considered to be a cash
payment, but the fiscal coupon may not be printed on paper, so instead, the seller may send
the fiscal coupon to the buyer in electronic form.
2. In the case of non-cash payments, for transactions with individual consumers, the fiscal
coupon can be printed on paper or sent in electronic form. The buyer must consent to
receiving the fiscal coupon in electronic form.
3. To ensure easy verification of the issued fiscal coupon, a QR Code must be generated for
each fiscal coupon. The QR Code is a mandatory element of the fiscal coupon, both in the
case when the fiscal coupon is successfully reported to TAK and fiscalized, and in cases
when the fiscal coupon cannot be fiscalized, such as: due to lack of internet, availability of
the fiscalization service server, etc. The QR Code contains fiscal coupon data, as well as the
digital signature.
4. The QR code, along with other mandatory elements of the fiscal coupon, should be an
integral part of each fiscal coupon issued to the final consumer, regardless of whether the
fiscal coupon is printed on paper or sent electronically to the consumer.
5. The software solution used for issuing fiscal coupons and their fiscalization must
automatically create a unique number in the format specified in the technical specifications
published on the TAK website.
Article 41
Fiscal Coupon Notification
1. The taxpayer, who issues fiscal coupons, is obliged to display a notice on each electronic
payment device or in another visible place in the taxpayer’s premises, indicating the

obligation to issue the fiscal coupon as well as the obligation of the buyer to receive and store
the fiscal coupon.
2. As an exception to paragraph 1 of this article, the taxpayer, who issues fiscal coupons, who
implements the procedure of fiscalization of sales through self-service devices (vending
machines), is obliged to present a notification for fiscalization of sales through these devices
at each self-service device.
3. The Kosovo Tax Administration shall determine the content and appearance of the notice
referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 of this article.
Article 42
Buyer’s obligations
The buyer or recipient of a fiscal coupon issued or received electronically must keep the fiscal
coupon issued or prove receipt of the fiscal coupon electronically, up to thirty (30) meters after
leaving the place of business of the seller, i.e. the taxpayer who issued the fiscal coupon. Upon
request of an authorized TAK official, the purchaser must show the fiscal coupon issued.
QR Code Example
MFLT
Article 43
Fiscal Coupon Verification
1. Buyers and all recipients of fiscal coupons, for all coupons received from taxpayers/persons
who are obliged to issue fiscal coupons, can verify whether the fiscal coupon is reported to
TAK.
2. Verification will be done by scanning the QR Code on the fiscal coupon, through a special

application, from a smartphone or any other electronic device that has the scanning function.
The QR Code creates a connection to the TAK Information System with details of the
scanned fiscal coupon. Here is an example of the QR code found on the fiscal coupon.
3. The purchaser and all recipients of the fiscal coupon can also perform the verification of the
fiscal coupon by making a request in the web browser or mobile phone application available
on the TAK website and manually entering the required data (for example, FCUIN). In
situations where the fiscal coupon is issued manually from the special fiscal coupon block
or when some of the mentioned data is missing, the purchaser can verify the fiscal coupon at
the nearest TAK office.
4. After scanning the QR Code or entering the data according to paragraph 3 of this article, the
page with details about the fiscal coupon opens, where there is an icon that indicates the
status of the fiscal coupon. There are possible cases, such as:
4.1. The fiscal coupon has been successfully registered; or
4.2. The fiscal coupon has a problem that needs to be reported.
5. If the fiscal coupon is successfully registered, information about the fiscal coupon issued is
displayed.
6. If the fiscal coupon has not been registered, a message will appear instructing the buyer to
verify later, this is because in some cases the fiscal coupon can be registered after it has been
issued, but the registration must be done within the time limit.
7. Sometimes an issued fiscal coupon needs to be corrected, so the taxpayer who issued the
fiscal coupon will issue a corrective fiscal coupon and fiscalize it by referring to the original
fiscal coupon. In this situation, the buyer will receive one of the following warning messages:
7.1. "Fiscalization successful, but this is a correction of another fiscal coupon" - In this
situation, the buyer has verified the corrective fiscal coupon and a reference to the
original fiscal coupon being corrected (its FCUIN) will appear. If the buyer has not
received the original fiscal coupon, to which this corrective fiscal coupon is linked,
then it is most likely that the taxpayer has committed a violation and the buyer must
report it to the TAK, directly in the request.
7.2. "Fiscalization successful, but there is a corrective fiscal coupon for this fiscal coupon"
- In this situation, the buyer has verified the original fiscal coupon and the reference
to his corrective fiscal coupon has been issued and fiscalized after the fiscal coupon
appears (his FCUIN). If the buyer has not received the corrective fiscal coupon, this
means that it is very likely that the taxpayer has committed a violation and the buyer
should report it to the TAK, directly on the application.
8. In case there is a problem with the fiscal coupon, which must be reported based on the fiscal
coupon verified by the buyer and the data stored on the TAK Information System, the

following situations may be encountered:
8.1. The fiscal coupon is not registered in the database - In this case, the fiscal coupon is not
found in the TAK database, which means that it has not been fiscalized, and more than
forty-eight (48) hours have passed since the issuance of the fiscal coupon and the
taxpayer does not have an approval for monthly fiscalization or the deadline for
subsequent fiscalization has not passed. In this case, TAK will conduct a detailed
analysis.
8.2. The Unique Identification Number of the Fiscal Coupon is not valid - In this case the
FCUIN is not formed properly and the error will appear. This error indicates that the
fiscal coupon is suspicious and should be further analyzed by TAK.
8.3. The issued fiscal coupon details do not match the fiscal coupon details in the request -
Possible reasons include a case where the fiscal coupon registration software contains
some errors and registers a fiscal coupon with incorrect data. Another reason could be
when the issued fiscal coupon has a fake code, FCUIN and QR Code, which are placed
on the fiscal coupon only to make it look correct, but in reality, they may be linked to
some other existing fiscal coupons.
8.4. The corrective fiscal coupon was not registered within the required deadline - In this
situation, the taxpayer has verified the corrective fiscal coupon, but this fiscal coupon
was not in the database, and more than forty-eight (48) hours have passed since the
issuance of the corrective fiscal coupon and the taxpayer does not have an approval
for the monthly fiscalization, or the deadline for the subsequent fiscalization has
passed.
8.5. Fiscal coupon parameters do not match the fiscal coupon details in the application - In
this situation, the QR Code on the issued fiscal coupon has a link to the online
application with query parameters that describe some of the important details for
identifying the fiscal coupon. These parameters include the FCUIN, the total amount,
the date of issuance of the fiscal coupon and other information. It is possible that the
QR Code contains invalid information, such as there is a fiscal coupon registered in
the FCUIN by the QR Code, but other parameters, such as the total amount of the
fiscal coupon, do not match the information on the fiscal coupon registered with the
same FCUIN.
9. If a verification request is officially incorrect; the buyer or recipient of the fiscal coupon
receives a message that it is necessary to check the accuracy of the information provided.
10. If the fiscal coupon verification system is temporarily unavailable, the purchaser or recipient
of the fiscal coupon receives a message stating that the fiscal coupon verification system is
temporarily unavailable.
11. If the fiscal coupon submitted for verification is not registered in the TAK database, or the
buyer encounters any other problem with the fiscal coupon, the buyer or recipient of the

fiscal coupon can report it directly in the “Denonco” application, which is published on the
TAK website. The buyer can also submit fiscal coupons with some problems or irregularities
to the nearest TAK office for control purposes.
Article 44
Fiscalization of correction/adjustment coupons
1. All obligations set forth in the provisions of this Instruction shall apply, as necessary, also to
the issuance of corrective/adjusting fiscal coupons.
2. The corrective/corrective fiscal coupon must contain a reference to the original fiscal coupon
being corrected.
Article 45
Disruption of electronic (internet) connection
1. In the event of interruption of the electronic connection (internet), the taxpayer issues a fiscal
coupon, which contains the data specified in Article 7 of this Instruction.
2. The taxpayer who issues fiscal coupons must, within forty-eight (48) hours, starting from the
moment of disconnection, establish an internet connection and submit to TAK all fiscal
coupons issued under paragraph 1 of this article.
3. Fiscal coupons issued under paragraph 1 of this Article are considered to have been fiscalized
correctly if the fiscalization process is completed within the deadline set out in paragraph 2
of this article.
4. In order for the process described in paragraphs 1 to 3 of this article to be an exception, the
taxpayer issuing fiscal coupons must provide appropriate software and hardware support for
the exchange and sending of data to TAK and appropriate electronic connections (internet).
5. If the taxpayer, who issues fiscal coupons, cannot, within the deadline specified in paragraph
2 of this article, restore the electronic connection (internet) due to the need to change the
equipment for establishing the electronic connection (internet), the inability to establish the
electronic connection (internet), for which the provider of this service is responsible, or due
to force majeure, he must notify TAK of this, electronically or in person at the relevant TAK
office where the taxpayer is registered, as well as send documentation or other evidence
proving the inability to establish the electronic connection (internet).
6. If it is impossible to connect within forty-eight (48) hours, after informing TAK in
accordance with paragraph 5 of this article, the taxpayer may continue to issue fiscal coupons
without a fiscalization procedure, until the connection is re-established. After the resumption
of operation, he/she fiscalizes all coupons issued in off-line mode. If the interruption in the
internet connection is encountered during the month and the reconnection is not made by the

tenth (10th) day of the following month, then the taxpayer must submit all unfiscalized fiscal
coupons to TAK.
Article 46
fiscalization implementation device
1. Fiscalization implementation device completely stops working , the taxpayer, who issues
fiscal coupons, issues a fiscal coupon from a separate block of fiscal coupons.
2. The taxpayer who issues fiscal coupons must fiscalize the special block of fiscal coupons at
TAK.
3. The separate fiscal coupon block must have a printed serial number for each fiscal coupon it
contains.
4. The special block of fiscal coupons contains two copies for each coupon: the original for the
consumer and the copy that remains with the supplier of the goods and/or services.
5. In the case referred to in paragraph 1 of this article, the taxpayer issuing fiscal coupons is
obliged, within forty-eight (48) hours from the moment the device has completely stopped
functioning, to enable the functioning of the device for issuing fiscal coupons or to provide
a new device.
6. Within five (5) days after the deadline set out in paragraph 5 of this article, the taxpayer who
issues fiscal coupons according to paragraph 1 of this article is obliged to register all fiscal
coupons in the EFS, which are automatically sent to the TAK Information System.
Article 47
Saving fiscal coupons
The taxpayer who issues fiscal coupons must take measures to preserve the issued fiscal coupons,
in accordance with the applicable Law on the Administration of Tax Procedures.
Article 48
Incentive to receive fiscal coupons
1. For all persons who wish to participate in the prize game using fiscal coupons, the form and
method of application is determined by TAK, after approval by the relevant Minister of
Finance.

2. The procedures for implementing this provision will be determined by a special Guideline
approved by the Director General of TAK, which defines the procedures for application,
electronic verification of fiscal coupons and selection of beneficiaries.
Article 49
Data protection
The provisions of this Instruction are implemented in compliance with the provisions of the law
in force on the protection of personal data.
Article 50
Fines and penalties
1. For non-implementation of legal provisions regarding fiscalization and issuance of fiscal
coupons, fines determined by the Law in force on the Administration of Tax Procedures are
applied.
2. The fines stipulated by the Law in force on the Administration of Tax Procedures, regarding
the failure to install, supply and maintain EFE/FS, apply accordingly to Electronic Fiscal
Software.
Article 51
Transitional provisions
1. The relevant Minister of Finance, by decision, determines the taxpayers and the period of
transition from the use of Electronic Fiscal Devices/Fiscal Systems to Electronic Fiscal
Software.
2. Taxpayers defined in Article 8 of this Instruction, who aren’t equipped with EFE/FS since
the entry into force of this Instruction, but they are obliged to install and use EFS within sixty
(60) days since this date. Within this period, these taxpayers are required to use fiscalized
fiscal coupons in TAK.
Article 52
Repealing Provisions
1. This Instruction repeals Administrative Instruction MF-No. 02/2018 on the Use of Electronic
Devices and Fiscal Systems.

2. Decisions issued by TAK in regard to EFS/FS, which aren’t in contradiction with this
Instruction, remains into force until the issuance of new decisions.
Article 53
Entry into force
This Instruction enters into force on the date of publication in the Official Gazette of the
Republic of Kosovo.
Hekuran Murati
______________________
Minister of Finance, Labor and Transfers
Date, ____/_____/ 2025

title: Udhezues per plotesimin e LIBRIT TE SHITJES
text:
Instructions for completing the SALES BOOK
Date: Enter the date of the invoice. The format should be: date / month / year, e.g. 02.01.2024. This column is
mandatory.
Invoice number: Enter the sale invoice number. The format is free, the column is mandatory.
Buyer name: Enter the full name of the buyer to whom the goods / services were supplied to, e.g. "XXXXX" L.L.C. The
format is free, the column is mandatory.
Unique Business Registration Number / Fiscal Number: Enter the Buyer's Unique Business Registration Number or Fiscal
Number of the buyer. Unique Business Registration Number – the format is 9 digits (persons who are registered in
KBRA), starts with 81... and contains only numbers. Fiscal Number - the format is 9 digits, starts with 6... and contains
only numbers (persons who are registered in TAK); In cases where the sales are from the Fiscal Electronic Device/Fiscal
System, Exports, the numbers should be set according to the table below. This column is validatable and is a
mandatory column.
Below you will find clarifications on how the UIN / FN column should be completed:
SALES BOOK
Putting the Number in the
Description
UIN/FN Column
UIN starting with 81... is set for Sales for persons registered in
81XXXXXXX
KBRA.
FN starting with 6…. It is set for Sales for persons who are
60XXXXXXX
registered in TAK.
Number 1 is set for Sales from the Fiscal Electronic Device /
1
Fiscal System, i.e. Daily Turnover.
Number 2 is set for Sales of services abroad, that is, if column b
2
is completed.
3 Number 3 is set for Exports, i.e. if column c is completed.
Zero is set in cases where the Sales Book is declared without
0
transactions, i.e. declaring the book with zero.
[a] Sales of goods/services within the country: Enter all sales of goods and services made within the country. The format
is number, it is not a mandatory column.
[b] Sale of services abroad: Enter all sales of services abroad. The format is a number, it is not a mandatory column.
[c] Exports: Enter all sales of goods abroad. The format is number, it is not a mandatory column.
[d] Total sales: Enter all sales by adding columns a + b + c. The format is a number, it is a calculating column, which
sums up the columns (d = a + b + c).
Attention: The Sales Book must be declared even if there are no transactions within the tax period. So the declaration
is made with zero (0).
